# [AArch64] Wrong code at -O2

**Author:** XChy
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/181002
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** miscompilation, vectorizers, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2026-02-12T22:17:56Z

## Body

Reproducer: https://godbolt.org/z/KosfxK9ff
Testcase:
```c
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <arm_neon.h>
#define BARRIER_v2u64(x) ((BS_VEC(uint64_t, 2))vqaddq_u64(x, (BS_VEC(uint64_t, 2)){}))
#define BS_VEC(type, num) type __attribute__((vector_size(num * sizeof(type))))
#define BITCAST(T, F, arg)                                                     \
    ((union {                                                                  \
        F src;                                                                 \
        T dst                                                                  \
    })arg)                                                                     \
        .dst
uint64_t BS_CHECKSUM;
void backsmith_checksum_4()
{
    int64_t BS_VAR_0 = 200366739780528840;
    uint64_t BS_VAR_3 = 0;
    int32_t BS_VAR_4 = 0;
    uint8_t BS_TEMP_479 =
        ~BITCAST(uint16_t, BS_VEC(uint8_t, 2),
                 __builtin_convertvector(BARRIER_v2u64((BS_VEC(uint64_t, 2)){}),
                                         BS_VEC(uint8_t, 2)));
    for (uint8_t BS_INC_0; BS_INC_0 < BS_TEMP_479; BS_INC_0 += 1)
    {
        BS_VAR_3 = BS_VAR_4;
        BS_VAR_4 = BS_INC_0;
        BS_VAR_0 = 0;
    }
    BS_CHECKSUM ^= BS_VAR_0 ^= BS_VAR_3 + 9;
}
int main()
{
    backsmith_checksum_4();
    printf("BackSmith Checksum = 0x%016llx\n", BS_CHECKSUM);
}
```
The output at -O1:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000106
```

The output at -O2:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000105
```

Looks like a recent regression as clang 21.1.0 cannot reproduce it.

```c
#include &lt;stdint.h&gt;
#include &lt;stdio.h&gt;
#include &lt;arm_neon.h&gt;
#define BARRIER_v2u64(x) ((BS_VEC(uint64_t, 2))vqaddq_u64(x, (BS_VEC(uint64_t, 2)){}))
#define BS_VEC(type, num) type __attribute__((vector_size(num * sizeof(type))))

    ((union {                                                                  \

        T dst                                                                  \
    })arg)                                                                     \

{

    uint64_t BS_VAR_3 = 0;
    int32_t BS_VAR_4 = 0;
    uint8_t BS_TEMP_479 =
        ~BITCAST(uint16_t, BS_VEC(uint8_t, 2),

        BS_VAR_4 = BS_INC_0;
        BS_VAR_0 = 0;

}
int main()
{
    backsmith_checksum_4();
    printf("BackSmith Checksum = 0x%016llx\n", BS_CHECKSUM);
}
```
The output at -O1:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000106

The output at -O2:
```
BackSmith Checksum = 0x0000000000000105

Looks like a recent regression as clang 21.1.0 cannot reproduce it.

---

### Comment 2 - XChy

It looks like a middle-end problem. A testcase without AArch64 intrinsics: https://godbolt.org/z/fef1febnG

---

### Comment 3 - davemgreen

It looks like the loop vectorizer does this:
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/gD9zWw
```llvm
define i64 @src(i64 %BS_CHECKSUM) {
entry:
  ;%vqaddq_v2.i.i = tail call <2 x i64> @llvm.aarch64.neon.uqadd.v2i64(<2 x i64> zeroinitializer, <2 x i64> zeroinitializer)
  %0 = bitcast <2 x i64> zeroinitializer to <16 x i8>
  %conv2.i = extractelement <16 x i8> %0, i64 0
  %cmp.peel.not.i = icmp eq i8 %conv2.i, -1
  br i1 %cmp.peel.not.i, label %backsmith_checksum_4.exit, label %for.cond.i.preheader

for.cond.i.preheader:                             ; preds = %entry
  %not.i = xor i8 %conv2.i, -1
  %wide.trip.count = zext i8 %not.i to i64
  br label %for.cond.i

for.cond.i:                                       ; preds = %for.cond.i.preheader, %for.cond.i
  %indvars.iv = phi i64 [ 0, %for.cond.i.preheader ], [ %indvars.iv.next, %for.cond.i ]
  %BS_VAR_3.0.i = phi i64 [ 0, %for.cond.i.preheader ], [ %indvars.iv, %for.cond.i ]
  %indvars.iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %indvars.iv, 1
  %exitcond.not = icmp eq i64 %indvars.iv.next, %wide.trip.count
  br i1 %exitcond.not, label %for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i, label %for.cond.i

for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i:                      ; preds = %for.cond.i
  %BS_VAR_3.0.i.lcssa = phi i64 [ %BS_VAR_3.0.i, %for.cond.i ]
  %1 = add nuw nsw i64 %BS_VAR_3.0.i.lcssa, 9
  br label %backsmith_checksum_4.exit

backsmith_checksum_4.exit:                        ; preds = %entry, %for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i
  %xor.i = phi i64 [ 200366739780528833, %entry ], [ %1, %for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i ]
  ;%2 = load i64, ptr @BS_CHECKSUM, align 8
  %xor12.i = xor i64 %BS_CHECKSUM, %xor.i
  ;store i64 %xor12.i, ptr @BS_CHECKSUM, align 8
  ;%call = tail call i32 (ptr, ...) @printf(ptr noundef nonnull dereferenceable(1) @.str, i64 noundef %xor12.i)
  ret i64 %xor12.i
}

define i64 @tgt(i64 %BS_CHECKSUM) {
entry:
  ;%vqaddq_v2.i.i = tail call <2 x i64> @llvm.aarch64.neon.uqadd.v2i64(<2 x i64> zeroinitializer, <2 x i64> zeroinitializer)
  %0 = bitcast <2 x i64> zeroinitializer to <16 x i8>
  %conv2.i = extractelement <16 x i8> %0, i64 0
  %cmp.peel.not.i = icmp eq i8 %conv2.i, -1
  br i1 %cmp.peel.not.i, label %backsmith_checksum_4.exit, label %for.cond.i.preheader

for.cond.i.preheader:                             ; preds = %entry
  %not.i = xor i8 %conv2.i, -1
  %wide.trip.count = zext i8 %not.i to i64
  %min.iters.check = icmp ult i64 %wide.trip.count, 2
  br i1 %min.iters.check, label %scalar.ph, label %vector.ph

vector.ph:                                        ; preds = %for.cond.i.preheader
  %n.mod.vf = urem i64 %wide.trip.count, 2
  %n.vec = sub i64 %wide.trip.count, %n.mod.vf
  br label %vector.body

vector.body:                                      ; preds = %vector.body, %vector.ph
  %index = phi i64 [ 0, %vector.ph ], [ %index.next, %vector.body ]
  %index.next = add nuw i64 %index, 2
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %index.next, %n.vec
  br i1 %1, label %middle.block, label %vector.body

middle.block:                                     ; preds = %vector.body
  %cmp.n = icmp eq i64 %wide.trip.count, %n.vec
  br i1 %cmp.n, label %for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i, label %scalar.ph

scalar.ph:                                        ; preds = %for.cond.i.preheader, %middle.block
  %bc.resume.val = phi i64 [ %n.vec, %middle.block ], [ 0, %for.cond.i.preheader ]
  %scalar.recur.init = phi i64 [ %index, %middle.block ], [ 0, %for.cond.i.preheader ]
  br label %for.cond.i

for.cond.i:                                       ; preds = %scalar.ph, %for.cond.i
  %indvars.iv = phi i64 [ %bc.resume.val, %scalar.ph ], [ %indvars.iv.next, %for.cond.i ]
  %BS_VAR_3.0.i = phi i64 [ %scalar.recur.init, %scalar.ph ], [ %indvars.iv, %for.cond.i ]
  %indvars.iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %indvars.iv, 1
  %exitcond.not = icmp eq i64 %indvars.iv.next, %wide.trip.count
  br i1 %exitcond.not, label %for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i, label %for.cond.i

for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i:                      ; preds = %middle.block, %for.cond.i
  %BS_VAR_3.0.i.lcssa = phi i64 [ %BS_VAR_3.0.i, %for.cond.i ], [ %index, %middle.block ]
  %2 = add nuw nsw i64 %BS_VAR_3.0.i.lcssa, 9
  br label %backsmith_checksum_4.exit

backsmith_checksum_4.exit:                        ; preds = %entry, %for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i
  %xor.i = phi i64 [ 200366739780528833, %entry ], [ %2, %for.cond.cleanup.loopexit.i ]
  ;%3 = load i64, ptr @BS_CHECKSUM, align 8
  %xor12.i = xor i64 %BS_CHECKSUM, %xor.i
  ;store i64 %xor12.i, ptr @BS_CHECKSUM, align 8
  ;%call = tail call i32 (ptr, ...) @printf(ptr noundef nonnull dereferenceable(1) @.str, i64 noundef %xor12.i)
  ret i64 %xor12.i
}
```

which simplify differently:
```llvm
define i64 @src(i64 %BS_CHECKSUM) local_unnamed_addr #0 {
  %xor12.i = xor i64 %BS_CHECKSUM, 262
  ret i64 %xor12.i
}

define i64 @tgt(i64 %BS_CHECKSUM) local_unnamed_addr #0 {
  %xor12.i = xor i64 %BS_CHECKSUM, 261
  ret i64 %xor12.i
}
```
